_comment: AstraSec FAQ — add/edit/remove Q&A pairs freely. 'id' must be unique. Order in this array = display order on page.

faqs.question: Who can participate in Legacy Secured?
faqs.answer: Legacy Secured is open to all students — undergraduate and postgraduate — from any college or university. Individual participants and teams of up to 4 members are welcome.

faqs.question: Is there a registration fee?
faqs.answer: Basic event access is free. Some specialized workshops and competitions may have a nominal entry fee, which will be announced at the time of registration.

faqs.question: Do I need to have prior cybersecurity experience?
faqs.answer: No prior experience is required for most events. We have beginner-friendly workshops and talks. The CTF and advanced competitions are suited for experienced participants.

faqs.question: What should I bring to the event?
faqs.answer: Bring your laptop with your preferred security tools pre-installed, a valid student ID, and your curiosity. Specific tool requirements will be shared in event-wise briefings.

faqs.question: How do I register for specific events or workshops?
faqs.answer: After completing general registration, you can sign up for individual events, workshops, and competitions through the event portal. Slots are limited and filled on a first-come basis.

faqs.question: Will certificates be provided?
faqs.answer: Yes. All participants will receive digital certificates of participation. Competition winners will additionally receive merit certificates and prizes.

faqs.question: Is accommodation available for outstation participants?
faqs.answer: Limited on-campus accommodation may be available. Please contact us at contact@astrasec.in well in advance to check availability and make arrangements.

faqs.question: Can I participate in the CTF remotely?
faqs.answer: The CTF qualifiers may be held online, but the finals will be conducted on-site only. More details will be shared closer to the event date.

faqs.question: How will winners be determined?
faqs.answer: Each event has its own scoring rubric. For CTF — points per flag. Workshops — participation-based. Tech Talks — judged by expert panel. Details are published per event.

faqs.question: How can I contact the organizing team?
faqs.answer: Reach us at contact@astrasec.in or through our social media channels on GitHub, LinkedIn, and Instagram. We aim to respond within 24–48 hours.
